title: acanthopleura involucrata
intro: Acanthopleura involucrata, also known as the "Kava Tree" or "Pacific Kava", is a plant species native to the Pacific Island region, specifically in the tropical and subtropical coastal areas of Australia, New Zealand, and the Pacific Islands. It is a shrub-like plant that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its sedative and anxiolytic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of kavalactones in its roots. The medicinal properties of Acanthopleura involucrata have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of depression and restlessness. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea, capsules, and tinctures, which can be consumed after consulting with a healthcare professional, as it may interact with certain medications or have adverse effects in high doses.
